column2: The transportation for almond milk is the dominant process with mor than 60% for energy use, fossil CO2 and CO2 eq emissions
The skimmed milk input during the production phase is the dominant source of energy use (38%) and  CO2 eq emissions (58.7%)

The almond milk scenario generates onl slightly lower environmental impacts in terms of energy use compared to the dairy milk scenario. However, the dairy milk scenario generates higher environmental impacts in terms of both fossil CO2 and carbon footprints compared to the almond milk scenario.

The ratios of gCO2/MJ fall within expected limits with manufacturing, processing, and transport by truck in the ~50-60s range. Disposal is either high for almond milk in the 70s or low for only waste paperboard at landfill in the 20s. Skimmed milk production is high at the 90s.
